summary: Plan is accurate and well-scoped. All referenced code locations verified against the codebase. Implementation order and approach are sound.

notes: smoothTreble EMA pattern confirmed at renderLoop.ts:81,99-101 — Step 1 mirrors this correctly
pointerDisturbance uniform push confirmed at particleField.ts:65-67 — Step 2 replaces it appropriately
pointerToDisturbance() confirmed at mappings.ts:166-170 — no changes needed
FrameState confirmed missing pointerX/pointerY at types.ts:3-10 — Step 3 adds them
evolution.ts passes pointerDisturbance through unchanged at line 114 — correctly left in files_unchanged
pointer.ts uses PointerEvent API (pointermove/pointerenter/pointerleave) — covers mouse+touch natively
Both test files already exist — Steps 4-5 add to existing files
MAX_PARTICLES=600 confirmed — spatial distance calc per frame is trivially cheap

implementation_order_assessment: Correct. Step 3 (thread pointer position) must precede Step 2 (spatial influence) since particleField needs pointerX/pointerY. Step 1 (EMA smoothing) is independent but logically first. Order [3,1,2,4,5,6] is valid.
risks_assessment: Both identified risks are reasonable. The 0.92 decay factor (~0.8s half-life) is a sensible starting point. Squared-distance optimization for 600 particles is unnecessary but harmless — even with sqrt, 600 distance calcs per frame is negligible.

suggestions.note: The existing treble EMA uses 0.85/0.15 (renderLoop.ts:100). Consider using a slightly faster attack than 0.08 (e.g., 0.12-0.15) to match the treble responsiveness while keeping the 0.92 decay. This prevents pointer input feeling sluggish on first movement.

Pointer x/y from pointer.ts are in viewport pixels. Normalize to 0-1 range (dividing by canvas width/height) in renderLoop.ts before passing to FrameState, not in particleField.ts, to keep the geometry system coordinate-agnostic.
